_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about important individuals and groups — including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples, local-born colonists, and migrants — who shaped the development of Australian society.

_source.w: We are learning about important individuals and groups — including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples, local-born colonists, and migrants — who shaped the development of Australian society.
y: Understanding the contributions of diverse individuals and groups gives us a richer picture of Australian history.
Recognising contributions from all backgrounds challenges the idea that only one group shaped the nation.
These stories inspire us to think about the contributions we can make to our own communities.
eg: Researching an Aboriginal leader who resisted colonisation or worked for the rights of their people.
Learning about individuals who shaped the gold rush era or the push for democracy.
Investigating the role of women in colonial society and the contributions they made.
Exploring how migrant communities established cultural traditions that still exist today.
Examining the contributions of a local historical figure to our community.
Comparing the contributions of an Aboriginal leader and a colonial leader from the same era.
